channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Tree needs pruning, 23rd June
item.description:
Tree needs pruning to ensure the road sign is visible to passing traffic.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 24, Queensway, Poverest, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Continued illegal parking, 23rd June
item.description:
This car show room parks cars on the road where there is yellow line every day which causes traffic issues on both sides of Bramley Road approaching Oakwood station and Chase Road. Why is this allowed when it is clearly in violation of the parking regulations. I reported this a month ago and these photos were taken yesterday, same car parked on the road. Is this because someone is paying the council/parking warden to turn a blind eye???

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Bramley Road, Southgate
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: New dropped kerb not flush with cycle path, 23rd June
item.description:
Dropped kerb reinstated following recent street works in the area. Now not flush with cycle path - circa 2cm difference. Lots of cyclists turning left from Albany Rd into Burgess Park here who will be approaching the kerb at an angle so high potential to lead to falls

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Albany Road, Walworth, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Flytipping AGAIN, 23rd June
item.description:
Rubbish being left outside constantly.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 114, Vestry Road, Camberwell, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Obstruction of pavement, 23rd June
item.description:
As above split bags of waste opposite 34 Milton Road.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Milton Road, Lessness Heath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Tree overhang, 23rd June
item.description:
Hedge and tree overhanging onto the footpath blocking peoples walkway. Bins don’t help either.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 144, Croxted Road, Dulwich, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Waste bins consistently left out on the street. Hazard for people walking by. Bins are never brought back into the property. Eye sore as well as a risk for any wheelchair users., 23rd June
item.description:
Bins sit on the street all the time. Creates a hazard for people walking by and wheelchairs struggle to get by. Not helped by overgrown hedge.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 144, Croxted Road, Dulwich, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Nappy on the road, 23rd June
item.description:
There’s a nappy on the road (not the pavement) between near number 10 Albion Square. It’s been there for over a week.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Albion Square, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: More flytiping all over road.. Close to econ house wall, 23rd June
item.description:
3rd large load of flytiping on road.. Nails and glass all over the place
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 1, Old Maidstone Road, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: No street name plaque, 23rd June
item.description:
Street name : “Bianca Road “ is missing on that road Please reattach the street name sign Thanx
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Bianca Road, Old Kent Road, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Very large tree overgrown blocking house light , low hanging for buses, 23rd June
item.description:
Tree is in corner of cavendish Road and Klea Avenue . All other trees on this road were neatly pollarded, why can’t this one also be tamed as it’s too big and leave will drip everywhere which block the drains and flood the road
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Klea Avenue, Clapham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipping of garden furniture, 23rd June
item.description:
Someone has fly tipped garden furniture under a tree and it looks terrible

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 65, Sherwood Park Avenue, Blackfen, Sidcup, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Pavement slabs higher in places, 23rd June
item.description:
Some paving slabs are at least an inch higher than adjoining ones making it a hazard for especially elderly people

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 60, High Street, Chislehurst, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Broken food waste communal bin, 23rd June
item.description:
The front panel of the communal food waste bin is broken. The smell is not contained, and foxes, rats and flies aren’t prevented from accessing the food waste bin.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Blanchard Way, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Bottom of ramp next to bench, 23rd June
item.description:
Drain cover missing very dangerous next to sheltered housing and community hall

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 108, Queen's Crescent, Gospel Oak, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Blocked drain, 23rd June
item.description:
I am reporting a blocked drainage issue again outside Rutland House building on Hawthorn Grove, Penge, as this is an ongoing problem that keeps happening. Following the torrential rain we had yesterday, everything has come back through and the drain now requires a thorough clean and flush out. There is currently no way for water to drain away properly during periods of heavy rainfall, which is causing repeated flooding and disruption. There is also a horrible smell that comes through randomly, even when it has not been raining. This unpleasant odour is becoming a regular occurrence and is causing concerns about hygiene and residents’ health and wellbeing. As this has become a recurring issue, I would be grateful if this could be properly investigated and permanently resolved rather than treated as a temporary fix. I have attached a photo showing the current situation for your reference. Please could you urgently look into this matter and arrange for the drainage system outside Rutland House building, Hawthorn Grove, Penge, to be thoroughly cleaned, flushed and cleared as soon as possible. Thank you for your attention to this matter .

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Hawthorn Grove, Anerley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Bags of rubbish dumped behind tree, 23rd June
item.description:
Bags of rubbish dumped behind tree, cans etc

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Lea Bridge Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: 202- 204 Lakedale Road SE18 1PU, 23rd June
item.description:
Blocked gully outside the houses on the common side.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Lakedale Road, Plumstead

item.title: Deep pothole on busy junction with Bishops Avenue. Difficult to get out and take a picture!, 23rd June
item.description:

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Lyttelton Road, Hampstead Garden Suburb, Greater London

item.title: Tree growth blocking pavement, 23rd June
item.description:
Tree growth blocking pavements, preventing access for wheelchairs/prams/pedestrians
